reference_types.implementation_variables: bin/stampede-merge.sh (SHADOW_SCORE, shadow_score, sealed test execution, percent calculation, level mapping, report JSON)
reference_types.documentation: All markdown files (README, AGENTS, BLOG, docs/story)

findings.model_aggregation: avg_score, best, worst per model stored in ~/.stampede/model-stats.json
gaps: Sealed test auto-generation not automated in pipeline
No minimum quality gates or rejection thresholds
Score justification text not implemented in shell script

findings.mechanism: Before launching agents, generate 3-5 acceptance tests per task, hash them with sha256sum stored in .seal-hash, never share with workers
findings.tamper_evidence: sha256sum hash verification of sealed tests before scoring
findings.worker_agent_awareness: stampede-agent.agent.md has NO mentions of sealed/envelope/hidden criteria (leaf workers are unaware by design)
findings.documentation: README.md and AGENTS.md both confirm: quality defined before agents run, measured silently after

gaps: No actual merge-report.json exists in current run dirs
No sealed-envelope tests in sample run (sealed-tests/ absent)
No per-agent JSON score output during execution
No markdown report generated, only JSON + console
Duplicate score logic between SKILL.md:700-768 and stampede-merge.sh

findings.non_link_reference: bin/stampede-merge.sh:233 (comment: # Interpret score per Shadow Score spec)
findings.implementation_type: external spec (linked) + internal implementation (embedded in skill, merger agent, and merge script)
findings.run_data_observation: No merge-report.json or score files found in existing .stampede run directories; result JSONs contain confidence/consensus/telemetry/launch_proof fields but not Shadow Score fields

synthesis.overall_status: Shadow Score integration is well-designed and substantially implemented but has key gaps between spec and live execution
strengths: External spec consistently linked (https://github.com/DUBSOpenHub/shadow-score-spec) across README, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, BLOG.md, docs
Spec URL is live and accessible
Complete 5-dimension weighted scoring formula implemented in bin/stampede-merge.sh
Sealed-envelope protocol fully specified in SKILL.md with tamper-evidence (sha256 hash)
Scorer is hidden from workers by design (stampede-agent.agent.md has no scoring awareness)
Score reporting schema defined in both agent spec and shell implementation
Atomic write for merge-report.json (tmp file + rename pattern)
Model-level aggregation and cross-run statistics implemented
Runtime bonus/penalty system accounts for speed and stuck events
gaps: No actual merge-report.json files exist in any .stampede run directory — scoring pipeline has not been exercised end-to-end
sealed-tests/ directory absent from all observed runs — sealed test generation not happening in practice
Score justification text (per-dimension explanations) specified in merger agent but not implemented in stampede-merge.sh
No minimum quality gates or auto-rejection thresholds
Duplicate score display logic between SKILL.md:700-768 and stampede-merge.sh (drift risk)
Existing run result files (commander-*.json, state.json) contain telemetry/confidence fields but no Shadow Score fields
synthesis.critical_finding: Shadow Score exists as a well-defined specification embedded in the codebase, but there is no evidence it has ever executed end-to-end in the current run infrastructure. The .stampede/ run directories lack both sealed-tests/ and merge-report.json, indicating the scoring pipeline is implemented but not yet integrated into the active orchestration flow.
recommendations: Wire sealed test generation into SKILL.md Step 5.5 with actual automated execution, not just prose instructions
Add merge-report.json to the run state schema so commander result bundles can reference it
Remove duplicate scorecard logic from SKILL.md or make it the single source of truth
Add a minimum score threshold (e.g., adjusted_total < 20 triggers rework signal)
Implement score justification text in stampede-merge.sh to match the merger agent spec
